title: Historical OMNISCIENCE is actually POSSIBLE!!! I can see it.

enrichment.summary: Kyle argues that 'historical omniscience' — memorizing all of history as covered on Wikipedia — is genuinely achievable with modern technology and self-directed study. He connects this to a broader proposal to slash societal overhead (schools, military spending, car culture) and redirect that energy toward a mass self-education movement, claiming he personally expects to demonstrate omniscience within a year or two by acing a random-history test. He frames this as both an economic fix for the coronavirus-era downturn and a long-term civilizational upgrade over autocratic systems like China.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Genuinely achievable by any motivated person using Wikipedia, audiobooks, and self-paced study
Should be abolished — it is expensive industrial-era babysitting, not real education
Cut overhead (schools, military, cars) rather than stimulus spending
More accurate than traditional encyclopedias because democratic editing beats autocratic curation
Free speech and individual freedom are prerequisites for a mass omniscience movement; autocracies cannot do it
A useful tool for absorbing and connecting knowledge
Should be pressured publicly to fund self-educated omniscience seekers; shamed if they refuse
A model of low-overhead communal living that produced strength and lasted a thousand years
Copied from Prussian industrial model in the 1870s and never updated — irrelevant today
